entry.title: Bitcoin covenants are inevitable
entry.summary:
The discussion on the economic utility derived from Bitcoin transactions highlights a crucial differentiation between velocity metrics and utility, emphasizing that these concepts should not be conflated. Velocity metrics involve the rate at which money changes hands, whereas utility pertains to the usefulness or value derived from holding or using Bitcoin. This distinction is important for understanding the economic implications of Bitcoin transactions.
Another interesting aspect touched upon is the practice of burning Bitcoin. Although this might seem counterintuitive given the finite nature of Bitcoin, there are legitimate scenarios where burning Bitcoin could be beneficial. These scenarios typically relate to the strategic removal of Bitcoin from circulation to manipulate its availability or value, potentially impacting the overall market dynamics.
